description: Writing past the end of a buffer, often an off-by-one from forgetting the NUL terminator or using <= in a bound.
body: Failure mode: a loop or string copy writes one or more bytes beyond the allocated size (e.g. strcpy with no room for '\0', or `for (i=0; i<=n; i++)`), smashing adjacent memory or the stack canary. Detection signals: ASan stack/heap-buffer-overflow; corrupted neighboring variables; crashes only with max-length input. Do not assume input fits or that a fixed buffer is large enough. Safer fallback: use sized operations (snprintf, memcpy with an explicit length, strlcpy), always reserve room for the terminator, validate input length first, and confirm with an ASan run on a boundary-length input.
